message:
fix(mcp/policy): workflow_run injector shares per-request rate limiter (ft-zo3cr)

Drift caught during ft-zo3cr architectural audit: at mcp_tools.rs:2558
the WaWorkflowRunTool handler built its injector's PolicyEngine via the
OLD `build_policy_engine(&config, ...)` helper, not the
shared-rate-limiter variant used for its own authorization engine at
:2494 and by every other build_policy_engine call site in the file.

Practical consequence: the workflow_run handler would (1) run the
per-request rate check at :2494 on the shared limiter, (2) pass, (3)
construct a FRESH PolicyEngine with a zero-state rate limiter for the
injector, and (4) let every injected send go through the fresh
limiter's empty budget — effectively doubling the allowed send rate for
any wa.workflow_run invocation. A missed spot in pane 2's ft-eu0no
SharedRateLimiter refactor.

Fix: swap to `build_policy_engine_with_shared_rate_limiter(&config, ...,
Arc::clone(&policy_rate_limiter))`. The handler already held
policy_rate_limiter in scope so the diff is mechanical — 4 lines.

Verified by grep: the only remaining `build_policy_engine(` match in
mcp_tools.rs is inside the comment I added documenting the old pattern.
All 9 other non-test call sites in this file now consistently use the
_with_shared_rate_limiter variant.

The bead's larger scope (centralize the repeated PolicyEngine +
PolicyGatedInjector + WorkflowEngine + WorkflowRunner assembly in
main.rs at :6992 / :15341 / :19850 / :25526) is out of this
reservation — filing ft-<new> as the main.rs follow-up where the
real constructor/config drift risk lives (e.g., :19850 includes
command_gate_config + policy_rules while :25526 does not).

Agent: RusticMeadow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2554,8 +2554,19 @@ impl ToolHandler for WaWorkflowRunTool {
 
                 let engine = WorkflowEngine::new(10);
                 let lock_manager = Arc::new(PaneWorkflowLockManager::new());
-                let injector_engine =
-                    build_policy_engine(&config, config.safety.require_prompt_active);
+                // ft-zo3cr: align the injector's PolicyEngine with the authorization
+                // engine at :2494 so BOTH share the same rate-limiter state for this
+                // request. Prior code built a fresh `build_policy_engine(...)` here,
+                // creating a second, independent rate limiter — the workflow run would
+                // pass the per-request rate check at :2494 but the injector's
+                // downstream sends would consult a zero-count limiter, effectively
+                // doubling the allowed send rate for any workflow_run invocation.
+                // Missed spot in pane 2's ft-eu0no SharedRateLimiter refactor.
+                let injector_engine = build_policy_engine_with_shared_rate_limiter(
+                    &config,
+                    config.safety.require_prompt_active,
+                    Arc::clone(&policy_rate_limiter),
+                );
                 let injector = crate::workflows::CxPolicyInjector::new(
                     PolicyGatedInjector::with_storage(
                         injector_engine,
